channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Trump signals he’s open to multiple budget bills instead of just one
item.description: Hours after the Senate approved a spending framework, President Donald Trump said in a Fox News interview that he’s open to approving the spending plan in multiple bills “as long as we get to the same point.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: US housing market worth nearly $50 trillion, over double a decade ago
item.description: The total value of all U.S. homes has doubled over the past decade. These cities saw the fastest growth last year.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis isn't out of danger but his condition isn't life-threatening, medical team says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis’ complex respiratory infection isn't life-threatening but he's not out of danger, his medical team said Friday, as the 88-year-old pontiff marked his first week in the hospital battling pneumonia in both lungs — along with bacterial, viral and fungal infections on top of his
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge cancels trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams but leaves corruption charges intact for now
item.description: A federal judge has adjourned the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department's request to drop charges against the Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Connor McDavid scores in OT to give Canada 3-2 win over United States in 4 Nations Face-Off final
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When they played “O Canada” for the second time, there were no American fans left in the arena to boo.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Crow is not the only thing they eat at a wild game dinner in rural Pennsylvania
item.description: PINE GROVE, Pa. (AP) — The buffet line inside the fire hall in rural Pennsylvania was a familiar sight last weekend as a crowd of about 150 people heaped dinner onto their plates before sitting down to eat, hear a little live music and wait for the raffle.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Trump's mass layoffs raise the risk of wildfires in the US West, according to fired workers
item.description: The termination letters that ended the careers of thousands of U.S. Forest Service employees mean fewer people and less resources will be available to help prevent and fight wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle And Claire Rush, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kids' disability rights cases stalled as Trump began to overhaul Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It was obvious to Christine Smith Olsey that her son was not doing well at school, despite educators telling her to leave it to the experts. The second-grade student stumbled over words, and other kids teased him so much he started to call himself “an idiot.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, COLLIN BINKLEY and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Funeral held for 2 Detroit kids who died in cold while family gets promise of a home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A group has stepped forward to provide a home for surviving family members of two Detroit children who died in a cold van in a casino parking garage, an official said. A funeral was held Thursday for Darnell Currie Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A flight to a Toronto paramedics conference becomes a real emergency for a former EMT
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former paramedic Peter Carlson is still having difficulty putting into perspective the shuddering jolt he and 79 others aboard Delta Air Lines flight 4819 experienced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu vaccine for chickens given conditional license from USDA
item.description: The company, Zoetis, said this license was granted on the “demonstration of safety, purity, and reasonable expectation of efficacy.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product is not currently available at stores amid the recall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: 23% of Americans ended a relationship over money: Survey
item.description: A new report shows nearly 1 in 4 Americans have ended a relationship because they weren't on the same page when it comes to finances.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Officials investigating bomb threat at Kennedy Center, police say
item.description: The Kennedy Center was evacuated on Thursday after a bomb threat was reported at the venue, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, NewsNation

item.title: IRS lays off 6,700 employees, torpedoing Democratic enforcement boost
item.description: The IRS fired 6,700 employees Thursday, a government official told NewsNation, the sister television network of The Hill. The employees were designated as probationary, meaning they were working for the agency on a trial basis prior to becoming full staff members.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, WGN 9

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2 people are dead in a small plane collision at a southern Arizona airport
item.description: A midair collision involving two small planes in southern Arizona killed two people Wednesday morning, authorities said. Federal air-safety investigators said each plane had two people aboard when they collided at Marana Regional Airport on the outskirts of Tucson.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: Survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Navy veteran's service dogs denied access on plane in Denver
item.description: A Navy veteran missed his flight after not being allowed to take his service dogs on a plane from Denver to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Shaul Turner, WGN 9

item.title: Black and blue, or white and gold? 'The dress' turns 10 years old
item.description: Ten years ago today, a debate started when a dress was posted on the internet asking what color people were seeing.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month into second term: Survey
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating appears to be underwater as many suggest he may be overstepping his authority, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, WGN 9

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KTLA 5

item.title: Viral video shows Indiana police punch man during arrest. Here's what we know
item.description: William Jerome Armstrong was arrested Tuesday afternoon in the parking lot of a Walmart after police say they saw his car parked in an emergency fire lane.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, Fox 5

item.title: New Social Security chief contradicts claims that millions of dead people are getting payouts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new head of the Social Security Administration said Wednesday that deceased centenarians are “not necessarily receiving benefits," contradicting claims that tens of millions of dead people over the age of 100 are getting payments from the agency.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Seven Chilean men are charged with burglarizing the homes of Mahomes, Burrow and other star athletes
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Seven men from Chile have been charged in Florida federal court with orchestrating burglaries at the homes of prominent professional athletes around the country, according to a criminal complaint.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: RFK Jr. eyes childhood vaccine schedule review, says 'nothing off limits' in HHS meeting
item.description: Kennedy urged his new staff to drop any preconceived notions of who he is, but also said he wouldn’t stop asking “difficult questions” that have led to “a lot of unpopular conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump's administration as the White House and its Department of Government Efficiency fire both new and career workers, tell agency leaders to plan for “large-scale reductions
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Get a text about a $1,400 IRS stimulus check? Don't click the link, BBB says
item.description: If you get a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The pope has pneumonia in both lungs but remains in good spirits, Vatican says
item.description: Francis was admitted to Rome’s Gemelli hospital in a “fair” condition on Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump will sign new executive orders while his first joint TV interview with Musk airs in prime time
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday was set to sign new executive orders from his Florida home while his first joint TV interview with adviser Elon Musk airs in prime time.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utahns participate in nationwide Presidents' Day protests against Trump, Musk
item.description: Hundreds of Utahns gathered at the State Capitol on Monday, Feb. 17, to participate in an hours-long protest against President Donald Trump — but not everyone at the Capitol shared the same viewpoint.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan May, ABC 4
